title: Angélique Porciani
text:
I enjoy working at the interplay of data, biology and real-world questions.

Contact
📧 angelique.porciani@gmail.com
GitHub
LinkedIn


Hello! I’m Angélique Porciani, a biostatistician and data analyst with over 12 years of experience working with complex data in life sciences.
Over the years, I’ve developed strong expertise in statistical modelling and data analysis with R, applied to a wide range of projects in epidemiology, health, and environmental sciences.
I particularly enjoy working with complex, time-dependent data, such as time series and longitudinal datasets, using statistical and machine learning approaches to explore patterns and support data-driven decisions. I also have experience with survival and dose-response models in the context of evaluating treatment efficacy.
More broadly, I’m interested in leveraging data to better understand complex systems and inform decision-making, whether in research or applied contexts. I’m especially motivated by projects where data can bring clarity to real-world challenges.
Beyond the technical side, I’ve coordinated projects, collaborated with diverse stakeholders, and communicated results to both technical and non-technical audiences. I enjoy working in interdisciplinary environments and value collaboration and knowledge sharing.
I’m currently seeking opportunities as a data analyst where I can apply my analytical skills to real-world data and contribute to impactful, data-driven projects.






🛠 Skills
Languages & Tools: R (tidyverse, dplyr, ggplot2), SAS, SQL, Excel, Latex, Quarto, Python, bash, Notion
Statistical Methods: descriptive statistics, regression linear or not, survival & dose-response modelling
Machine Learning: variable selection, clustering, predictive modelling
Data Visualization & Reporting: dynamics html report (Quarto), KPI creation, summary tables, clear visual communication
Soft Skills: collaboration, teaching, project coordination, interdisciplinary teamwork



Current Focus

Cleaning, transforming, and exploring complex datasets for actionable insights
Creating clear visualizations, dashboards, and indicators for decision-making
Applying statistical modelling & machine learning to real-world biological data
Time-dependent data: time series, longitudinal datasets
Survival & dose-response analyses to evaluate new molecules or interventions

title: ANIVERMATE
text:
Context
The use of endectocides such as ivermectin as an additional malaria control tool is now receiving increased attention, driven by its different mode of action compared to insecticides used so far and its excellent safety record for humans. In this project, we assess the efficacy against mosquitoes of a long lasting formulation of Ivermectin injected in cattle and its fate in environment.
Objectives
Characterization of PK properties of a new long acting formulation of Ivermectin and efficacy assessment against Anopheles gambiae and An. coluzzii. Estimation of the impact on non target fauna and fate in sediment.
Methods

Data management, data quality control and cure
Non compartmental PK model for parameters estimations : AUC, Tmax, \(t_{1/2}\)…
Generalized linear mixed models (binomial distribution)
Survival : cox, kaplan meier.

Tools
- R (tidyverse, ggplot2, dplyr, glmmTMB, survival, coxme, PKNCA)
Key Results

Long-acting ivermectin formulation tested can sustain mosquitocidal effects for >6 months.
Ivermectin-treated cattle can significantly reduce mosquito survival and population size
Livestock-based vector control targets zoophagic and opportunistic malaria vectors
There are measurable ecotoxicological risks to non-target dung fauna and soil ecosystems

Impact
Sustained mosquitocidal activity achieved through long-acting ivermectin formulations supports durable vector control strategies while reducing the need for frequent interventions. By targeting zoophagic and opportunistic vectors via treated livestock, this approach complements human-centered measures and addresses residual transmission. The resulting decrease in mosquito survival and population density is expected to contribute to reduced malaria transmission at the community level. However, the identification of ecotoxicological risks underscores the importance of developing environmentally sustainable implementation frameworks that balance intervention efficacy with ecosystem integrity. (Heinrich et al. (2024)).
Github link of the project : Anivermate




References

Heinrich, Andre Patrick, Sié Hermann Pooda, Angélique Porciani, Lamidi Zéla, Alexandra Schinzel, Nicolas Moiroux, Christophe Roberge, et al. 2024. “An Ecotoxicological View on Malaria Vector Control with Ivermectin-Treated Cattle.” Nature Sustainability 7 (6): 724–36. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41893-024-01332-8.

title: ANORYTHM
text:
Context
“Right time define a good vector”. Indeed, the activity rhythm is crucial for the transmission. In this project, we had studied the impact of different parameters on rhythm of Anopheles coluzzii, from mutation to exposition to light.
Objectives
Estimation of parameters that characterize the rhythm of mosquitoes as period, onset time, maximum activity time … and compare them between group. Other parts of the project was to test a data driven approach to estimate probability of being in activity or not and decompose activity in different level of activity.
Methods

Data management, data quality control and cure
Chi square periodogram, creation of specific function for automatisation of parameter estimation as onset time, maximum activity time …
Generalized linear mixed models with zero inflation
Hidden Markov models (HMM)

Tools
R (tidyverse, ggplot2, dplyr, glmmTMB, broom.mixed, xsp, momentuHMM)
Key Results

Insemination and gonotrophic status alter locomotor activity rhythms.
Inseminated females maintain activity during the subjective day under DD conditions.
Suggests possible daytime activity of A. coluzzii under low-light conditions.
A data-driven framework enables the estimation of activity probability and intensity over time. Hidden Markov Models (HMM) allow identification of distinct activity states and their temporal probabilities. These approaches facilitate assessment of how biological and environmental factors influence specific activity states, improving understanding of rhythm modulation.

Impact
Potential residual malaria transmission during daytime. This project advanced our understanding of how Anopheles coluzzii activity rhythms respond to both external and internal variability.(Traoré et al. (2021)).




References

Traoré, Amadou S., Angélique Porciani, Nicolas Moiroux, Roch K. Dabiré, Frédéric Simard, Carlo Costantini, and Karine Mouline. 2021. “Effects of Insemination and Blood-Feeding on Locomotor Activity of Wild-Derived Females of the Malaria Mosquito Anopheles Coluzzii.” Parasites & Vectors 14 (1): 457. https://doi.org/10.1186/s13071-021-04967-0.

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text: This project aimed to evaluate new long-acting injectable formulations of ivermectin as a complementary strategy for malaria vector control. A key challenge was extending the duration of efficacy beyond current limitations (<7 days).

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text: This project aimed to evaluate new long-acting injectable formulations of ivermectin as a complementary strategy for malaria vector control. A key challenge was extending the duration of efficacy beyond current limitations (<7 days).

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text:
Objectives

Identify the optimal formulation and dose meeting WHO Preferred Product Characteristics (HR > 4 for at least one month)
Evaluate efficacy in semi-field conditions (cattle–wild mosquitoes)
Estimate the epidemiological impact on malaria prevalence
Assess resistance development in Anopheles and Aedes
Study potential behavioural effects on mosquito host-seeking

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text:
My role

Designed study protocols, including sample size estimation and power calculations
Developed and implemented the statistical analysis plan
Performed data management, cleaning, and quality control
Conducted statistical analyses (survival models, GLMM, GAM, PK/PD, dose-response; epidemiological model)
Supervised students and supported partners in data analysis and interpretation
Communicated results to diverse stakeholders (scientific and non-scientific audiences)

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text:
Methods and tools

Survival analysis, GLMM, GAM, PK/PD modelling, non-linear dose-response, epidemiological model SAP, DMP redaction, study design, sample size estimation, power calculation, indicator of efficacy creation
R (tidyverse, ggplot2, glmmTMB, coxme, mgcv, drc, PKNCA)
Data management and reporting (R, Excel, Word, Quarto)
Study design, sample size estimation, power calculation, indicator of efficacy creation

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text:
Key Results

Sustained mosquitocidal efficacy for at least two months with HR > 4, meeting WHO PPC
Repeated treatment campaigns significantly reduced malaria prevalence (up to ~78%)
Evidence of differential resistance between Aedes and Anopheles
No significant effect on host-seeking behaviour

title: IMPACT – Evaluation of long-acting ivermectin formulations for malaria control
text:
Impact
Contributed to the development of a novel malaria control strategy aligned with WHO recommendations Supported the selection of a lead formulation, enabling progression to Phase I clinical trials Provided key insights for deployment strategies, including resistance management

title: Insecticides evaluation
text:
Context
Test of new type of insecticides on mosquitoes mortality
Objectives
- Choose of lethal dose (LD) 50 and 90 for different mosquitoes strains (different mutation conferring partial resistance to insecticides)
Methods

Dose response
Survival model : Kaplan meier curves and cox
Generalized linear models

Tools
- R (tidyverse, ggplot2, dplyr, glmmTMB, DHARMa, coxme, survival, drm, BioRssay)
Key Results
- Identification of efficient dose for 2 news type of insecticides.
Impact
Support for development of news control vector tools by a private company.

title: Larvae Movement
text:
Context
Environment is more and more polluted by insecticides, particularly in water collection where mosquitoes larvae developed themselves. The quantity found in these places could be insufficient to kill mosquitoes larvae, but could have indirect effect on movement or other life history traits. Moreover, these effect could be observed in adult mosquitoes later and have impact on traits and in malaria transmission.
Objectives

Develop a set up low cost, usable on field condition that allow video recording of multiple larvae
Develop an application that allow tracking of larvae
Analysis of larvae movement and adult activity from univariate to multivariate analysis and use of HMM for activity characterization of larvae and adult

Methods

Study design, sample size, dose choice, experiment realisation
Data collection, management, data quality control, cure and storage (FAIR principle)
Chi square periodogram, creation of specific function for automatisation of parameter estimation as onset time, maximum activity time …
Univariate and multivariate analysis
Hidden Markov models (HMM)

Tools
R (tidyverse, ggplot2, dplyr, glmmTMB, broom.mixed, xsp, momentuHMM, PCA)
Key Results
Data analysis is still ongoing, but some promising results show a difference of response between exposed and non exposed mosquitoes larvae and adults…

title: RADO
text:
Ce projet est un projet un peu particulier car il n’est pas question que de données mais des humains qui travaillent avec. C’est donc la création d’un groupe d’animation autour des biostatistiques et de la data au sein de deux unités de recherche de l’IRD. C’est un projet est né d’un du constat que les personnes travaillant sur les données sur le centre IRD de Montpellier ne se connaissaient pas et que l’on aurait tous à y gagner de changer ça. J’ai donc pris l’initiative avec un collègue d’une autre unité de lancer le groupe Biostat en 2022. Au bout d’un an, le groupe s’est agrandit et a fusionné avec un autre groupe plus centré sur la bio-informatique et cela a conduit à la création du RADO que j’ai également co-animé jusqu’à mon départ. L’animation de ce groupe a été une joie pour moi et m’a permis de monter en compétences, mais surtout des moments de partage de connaissances et de sympathie.
Ce réseau est toujours actif et s’agrandit grace au travail des animateurs et animatrices. Jetez y un oeil ici => RADO

title: Angélique Porciani
text:
I enjoy working at the interplay of data, biology and real-world questions.

Contact
📧 angelique.porciani@gmail.com
GitHub
LinkedIn


Hello! I’m Angélique Porciani, a biostatistician and data analyst with over 12 years of experience working with complex data in life sciences.
Over the years, I’ve developed strong expertise in statistical modelling and data analysis with R, applied to a wide range of projects in epidemiology, health, and environmental sciences.
I particularly enjoy working with complex, time-dependent data, such as time series and longitudinal datasets, using statistical and machine learning approaches to explore patterns and support data-driven decisions. I also have experience with survival and dose-response models in the context of evaluating treatment efficacy.
More broadly, I’m interested in leveraging data to better understand complex systems and inform decision-making, whether in research or applied contexts. I’m especially motivated by projects where data can bring clarity to real-world challenges.
Beyond the technical side, I’ve coordinated projects, collaborated with diverse stakeholders, and communicated results to both technical and non-technical audiences. I enjoy working in interdisciplinary environments and value collaboration and knowledge sharing.
I’m currently seeking opportunities as a data analyst where I can apply my analytical skills to real-world data and contribute to impactful, data-driven projects.






🛠 Skills
Languages & Tools: R (tidyverse, dplyr, ggplot2), SAS, SQL, Excel, Latex, Quarto, Python, bash, Notion
Statistical Methods: descriptive statistics, regression linear or not, survival & dose-response modelling
Machine Learning: variable selection, clustering, predictive modelling
Data Visualization & Reporting: dynamics html report (Quarto), KPI creation, summary tables, clear visual communication
Soft Skills: collaboration, teaching, project coordination, interdisciplinary teamwork



Current Focus

Cleaning, transforming, and exploring complex datasets for actionable insights
Creating clear visualizations, dashboards, and indicators for decision-making
Applying statistical modelling & machine learning to real-world biological data
Time-dependent data: time series, longitudinal datasets
Survival & dose-response analyses to evaluate new molecules or interventions
